Platform at a Glance

ADSNPP: Evidence-Based Nuclear Governance

A fully open-source platform transforming reactor technology selection from a qualitative, vendor-driven process into a transparent, auditable, and reproducible analytical framework for newcomer nuclear countries.

Publication Figures

Simulation Outputs & Analysis

Fig. 1Monte Carlo Distributions & Rank Probability Matrix
Parallel Monte Carlo uncertainty quantification (N=10,000). Left: violin plots; white dots=mean; bars=95% CI. Right: rank probability matrix. BWRX-300 holds Rank 1 with 97.2% probability.
Fig. 2Parallel Speedup — CPU and GPU
Speedup curves vs Amdahl prediction (f=0.08). A100 GPU achieves 52.6x speedup enabling real-time re-computation.
Fig. 3MCDA Criterion Profile Radar Charts
Six-dimension criterion profiles for top-3 ranked technologies. BWRX-300 leads on CE; HTR-PM excels on Safety and Proliferation Resistance.
Fig. 4N-CEF v2 Circular Economy Scoring
Per-dimension N-CEF scores (left) and composite CEI after LCA penalty (right). Both Gen IV and SMR exceed the 0.70 CE threshold.
Fig. 5ADSNPP Five-Layer Architecture
Five-layer open-source architecture with REST/gRPC interfaces for integration with existing nuclear simulation tools.
Fig. 6Tornado Sensitivity Analysis
One-at-a-time +/-10% weight perturbation. BWRX-300 retains Rank 1 across all perturbations. Largest sensitivity from Waste/CE and LCOE.

Open-Source Code

Core Parallel TOPSIS Implementation

The decision engine at the heart of ADSNPP. Available under EUPL v1.2.

# adsnpp/core/decision_engine.py
import numpy as np
from concurrent.futures import ProcessPoolExecutor

def topsis_rank(D, weights, benefit_flags):
  norms = np.sqrt((D**2).sum(0))
  R = D / np.where(norms==0, 1e-9, norms)
  V = R * weights
  A_best = np.where(benefit_flags, V.max(0), V.min(0))
  A_worst = np.where(benefit_flags, V.min(0), V.max(0))
  D_best = np.sqrt(((V - A_best)**2).sum(1))
  D_worst = np.sqrt(((V - A_worst)**2).sum(1))
  return D_worst / (D_best + D_worst)

def parallel_monte_carlo(D, U, weights, benefit_flags, N=10000):
  """Vectorised Bayesian uncertainty quantification."""
  rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
  noise = rng.standard_normal((N, *D.shape)) * U
  samples = np.clip(D[np.newaxis] + noise, 0, 1)
  scores = np.array([topsis_rank(s, weights, benefit_flags) for s in samples])
  lo, hi = np.percentile(scores, [2.5, 97.5], axis=0)
  return scores.mean(0), np.stack([lo, hi], axis=1)
